title: Plotting your data
intro: A plot is how you SEE data. matplotlib draws lines, scatters, bars and histograms — and this homework is as much about choosing the RIGHT plot and reading one honestly as drawing it. Work through it, then check in.
preview:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.plot(months, yields)
plt.xlabel("month"); plt.ylabel("yield %")   # a plot with no labels is useless
plt.show()
question: The notebook plots 12 months of reaction yields. How many months had a yield above the average? (the check-in prints it)
